Situation and measures in place

## Outbreaks in the Westhoek

- o Phylogenetic analysis:
- o No connection between the Westhoek outbreaks in poultry and wild birds sampled in the same region at this period
- o The strains from outbreaks 1 to 10 in Westhoek in Belgium and in Warhem (France) are close related -> CLUSTER

## o Hypothesis

- o No direct or indirect contacts between the outbreak farms
- o Warhem (FR) could be a consequence of Veurne 1 (BE) or originate from the same source (wind from the north-west)
- o The following outbreaks in the Westhoek region in Belgium could be secondary outbreaks caused by the accumulation of the virus in this area and its spread by the wind

<!-- image -->

Poultry outbreaks in WVL Westhoek + Deerlijk + Nov. dec. 25 wild birds, Belgium + fox Gent.

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry Deerlijk

- o Deerlijk: Province of West Flanders
- o Commercial farm with 26,491 meat turkey
- o Confirmation outbreak: 25 th January 2026
- o Increased mortality
- o Source: Independent primarily introduction from the Westhoek cluster

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in hobby holder Tielt

- o Tielt: Province of West Flanders
- o Hobby holder with 16 captive birds (6 hens and 10 ducks)
- o Confirmation outbreak: 25 th January 2026
- o Increased mortality in five ducks
- o Source : contact with wild birds which frequented the pond on the site where the captive birds were raised

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

Here is a map of Belgium and Belgium, with a note that it is labeled with the letters A26.

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 Measures in captive birds and Poultry

- o Culling of all captive birds and poultry in all the outbreaks
- o Litter removed, cleaning and disinfection
- o Protocol for restocking with at least a 3-week sanitary vacuum
-  Repopulation after approval by the FASFC
- o Establishment of Protection (3 km) and Surveillance (10 km) zones around all outbreaks in poultry
- o Outbreak in captive birds in Tielt: measures only applied in the establishment and no zoning considering:
- o derogation in Commission Delegated Regulation 2020/687/EU
- o type and the small size of the establishment (16 captive birds)
- o localisation (hobby holder isolated and without contacts)
- o increased risk period still implemented in Belgium for professional and hobby holders
- o intensive surveillance still in force for poultry, captive birds and wild birds

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreak of HPAI H5N1 in poultry Additional measures in the whole territory - Increased risk period

- o Increased risk period declared on 15 November 2021: measures adapted following the epidemiological situation -> measures reinforced on the 23 rd October 2025 :
-  Mandatory confinement or protection of all poultry and captive birds of registered holdings
-  Mandatory drinking and feeding inside or protected for poultry and captive birds
-  Prohibition of watering of poultry and captive birds with surface water or rainwater
- o Increased daily biosecurity
- o Increased vigilance
- o Gatherings of poultry and captive birds already forbidden except for hobby poultry noncommercial gatherings

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreak of HPAI H5N1 in poultry Additional measures in the restricted zone Veurne-Alveringem

- o Increased vigilance in poultry farms since 13 January

 If 10 more birds are found dead in a barn or a compartment:

-  The farm veterinarian applies a quick test for AI, or
- he/she sends samples for HPAI diagnostics to the lab
-  The farm veterinarian contacts the local control unit of the FASFC by telephone and confirms his findings by e-mail

<!-- image -->

## Outbreak of HPAI in wild birds 1/10/2025 until 13/02/2026

o 412HPAI H5 cases  362 H5N1 HPAI  50 H5NX HPAI o 173 different sites
